llm_eval.what_worked: Agent identified a measurable edge (22.5%) and had conviction to execute; the trade resulted in significant profit (+150%), suggesting the directional bias was correct.
llm_eval.what_to_improve: Critical information is missing: no entry thesis, no LLM confidence level, no documented reasoning. This prevents proper process evaluation. For future trades, record: the specific catalyst or technical signal, confidence percentage, position sizing rationale, and exit criteria before entry.
llm_eval.lesson: A win without documented process cannot validate trading skill—luck and edge are indistinguishable without reasoning records.
llm_eval.avoid_next_time: Never execute trades without recording: (1) explicit thesis, (2) confidence level, (3) reasoning chain. Avoid relying on memory; written documentation is essential for iterative improvement and distinguishing edge from variance.

llm_eval.what_worked: Cleveland Fed CPI nowcast showed shelter component at 4.21% with 6-month momentum. Kalshi implied only 15% probability vs model's 40%. Edge was +25 points — exactly the type of systematic mispricing the engine is designed to find.
llm_eval.what_to_improve: Sizing was conservative at ~$45 when edge warranted $100-150. Need to trust the model sizing formula on high-conviction ECONOMIC_DATA plays.
llm_eval.lesson: High-edge economic data plays deserve full sizing. +25pt edge on a fundamental data signal is a high-conviction setup.
llm_eval.avoid_next_time: Don't undersize when quant edge is strong and thesis is sound. Cost: left significant PnL on the table.

raw_thesis: BLS shelter CPI has documented 6-month momentum. Prior reading 4.21%. Model: 40% probability above 4.24% threshold. Market implied 15%.

llm_eval.what_worked: GDPNow data model predicted GDP well below 2.0% threshold. Market underpriced NO probability. Edge was 40+ points. Held conviction through volatile period.
llm_eval.what_to_improve: Could have sized larger — strong fundamental signal with months of confirmation.
llm_eval.lesson: Economic data models work. GDPNow called a negative GDP quarter months in advance.
llm_eval.avoid_next_time: Do not exit early on strong fundamental positions due to short-term market noise.

raw_thesis: GDPNow 1.31% << 2.0% threshold. 49 NO contracts. GDP came in between 1.5-2.0%, resolved NO. +$25.01 on $23.98.
raw_llm_reason: Strong thesis: GDPNow well below threshold, multiple fundamental headwinds confirmed.

llm_eval.what_worked: Nothing — execution went opposite to thesis.
llm_eval.what_to_improve: Direction enforcement must be a hard check at execution time, not advisory. Thesis direction lock was not enforced for this ticker at time of entry.
llm_eval.lesson: A correct thesis with wrong direction is a loss. The edge calculation and the execution direction must be verified as consistent before any order is placed.
llm_eval.avoid_next_time: Never place a trade without confirming side == thesis direction. Thesis lock must be enforced in code, not just documented.

raw_thesis: BUG: Entered YES instead of NO. 54 YES contracts @ ~32c. GDP did not exceed 2.5%, YES paid $0. Full loss -$17.49.
display_title: Q1 GDP > 2.5% (Apr 30) — BUG: Entered YES, should be NO
notes: Execution bug — entered YES instead of NO. GDP did not exceed 2.5%, YES paid $0. Loss -$17.49.

llm_eval.what_worked: Same GDPNow thesis as T2.0. Strong fundamental signal, correct direction, held to resolution.
llm_eval.what_to_improve: Sizing could have been larger on highest-confidence tier.
llm_eval.lesson: Stack positions across thresholds when fundamental signal is strong and correlated.

raw_thesis: GDPNow 1.31% << 3.0%. 33 NO contracts. GDP resolved NO. +$2.30 on $28.57 (5% return — entered at high NO price, slim margin).

Hormuz Strait normalization by May 1 — Iran actively seizing ships, collecting tolls, blockade ongoing. Pentagon: 6 months minimum to clear mines. ~8 ships/day vs 100+ normal. No path to normalization by deadline.

llm_eval.what_worked: Thesis was correct — no deal happened.
llm_eval.what_to_improve: Same direction bug as KXGDP-T2.5. Execution entered YES on a NO thesis. Direction enforcement patch was applied after these trades.
llm_eval.lesson: Two wrong-direction entries in the same period confirm a systemic execution bug, not isolated incidents. Patching the edge filter also patched direction enforcement.
llm_eval.avoid_next_time: Verify direction at execution gate. Thesis must explicitly state BUY YES or BUY NO and code must check it matches before submitting.

raw_thesis: BUG: Entered YES at ~8c instead of NO. 375 YES contracts. No deal happened, market resolved NO. YES paid $0. Full loss -$30.

llm_eval.what_worked: Clear quantitative edge (15%) with directional thesis backed by model forecasting. Temperature bracket predictions are measurable, discrete outcomes with good historical data. Entry price of 47c on a NO position with 0 payout if correct shows favorable risk/reward setup. Agent appropriately sized entry (10 contracts) and had pre-defined model basis for conviction.
llm_eval.what_to_improve: Critical gap: LLM reasoning at entry was not recorded. This prevents post-hoc analysis of whether conviction held through outcome. Need explicit documentation of model confidence metrics (probability estimate), forecast confidence intervals, and exit rules before entry. Should also document how the 15% edge was calculated relative to market price implied probability.
llm_eval.lesson: Strong process requires recording decision rationale contemporaneously; outcome bias makes post-hoc reconstruction unreliable for learning.
llm_eval.avoid_next_time: Avoid entering any discretionary trade without documented model forecast specifics (not just direction, but probability %), confidence intervals, and decision rules. The +112% win masks potential overconfidence—verify the edge calculation was sound, not lucky pricing.

raw_thesis: Austin 91-92F bracket NO. Model: forecast well below 91F. Entry: 10 contracts @ 47c. Payout: 0 per contract if NO wins.

llm_eval.what_worked: Trade had a clearly quantified edge (15%) and defined weather forecast model as the basis. Position sizing was reasonable (20 contracts). Entry logic was explicit and testable against actual outcomes.
llm_eval.what_to_improve: Critical failure: no documentation of LLM reasoning at entry. Cannot audit whether the 15% edge was validated by model outputs or was assumed. Need to verify: (1) actual forecast probability vs market odds at entry (24c), (2) whether model had recent performance data, (3) confidence intervals around the forecast. The 82-83F bracket resolution suggests model may have been miscalibrated or basis for edge was not sufficiently tested.
llm_eval.lesson: A quantified edge without documented model justification is speculation dressed as analysis—always record the specific forecast probability and how it compares to market implied probability before entry.
llm_eval.avoid_next_time: Do not enter weather prediction markets without: (1) explicit model forecast probability logged, (2) comparison of model prob vs market odds to confirm edge math, (3) recent backtesting or validation of model performance on similar conditions. The direct loss to actual outcome (forecast below bracket, but bracket occurred) suggests either model error or oversimplified edge calculation.

raw_thesis: Phoenix 82-83F bracket NO. Model: forecast below bracket. Entry: 20 contracts @ 24c. Phoenix actually hit 82-83F — market resolved YES against our NO.

llm_eval.what_worked: Quantified edge (15%) provided some analytical rigor; directional conviction was stated clearly
llm_eval.what_to_improve: Critical missing documentation: (1) LLM reasoning completely unrecorded despite LLM involvement, (2) exposure size not specified in thesis, (3) no exit criteria or loss limits defined, (4) no weather data or meteorological basis provided for a 'weather position', (5) no tail-risk assessment for a binary outcome instrument
llm_eval.lesson: A quantified edge without documented reasoning and exposure discipline is speculation, not systematic trading—never approve a trade without recording the full decision chain.
llm_eval.avoid_next_time: Avoid: taking weather derivative positions (binary, high-vol) without explicit meteorological forecasts, entry rationale, and pre-defined loss limits; approving any trade missing LLM reasoning trace; treating 'exposure=' as complete documentation of position sizing

llm_eval.what_worked: Agent identified a directional view (NO) with a measurable forecast (71.9F) and quantified edge (54.5%). Trade had explicit market-to-model comparison.
llm_eval.what_to_improve: Critical red flag ignored: model probability of 0.05 (5%) for the event contradicts betting NO at 59 cents mid-price. At 59 cents, market implies ~59% probability - agent's model says only 5%. This 54-point mismatch suggests either model miscalibration or fundamental disagreement not resolved. Should require explicit reconciliation of model vs market before entry.
llm_eval.lesson: When your model probability and market probability diverge dramatically (5% vs 59%), don't trade the edge—investigate the source of disagreement first.
llm_eval.avoid_next_time: Avoid relying solely on entry_edge percentage when the underlying model-market disagreement appears extreme or unexplained. Missing LLM confidence and reasoning at entry enabled this oversight.

llm_eval.what_worked: Agent identified significant discrepancy between model probability (3%) and market price (46% implied), creating a 43.4% quantified edge. The forecast of 98.1F was well below the 92.5F strike, providing fundamental support for the NO position. Directional conviction was mathematically justified.
llm_eval.what_to_improve: Lack of recorded LLM confidence and reasoning creates blind spots for process review. Document confidence level at entry to enable calibration analysis. Consider stress-testing: what weather model uncertainty or forecast drift would invalidate the thesis? Add explicit confidence bands around the 98.1F forecast.
llm_eval.lesson: Strong mathematical edge (43.4%) with clear model-vs-market divergence is a reliable process signal regardless of LLM confidence documentation gaps.
llm_eval.avoid_next_time: Don't let missing confidence metadata excuse incomplete reasoning capture—this prevents learning from edge degradation or tail events. Avoid relying solely on point forecasts (98.1F) without confidence intervals or model ensemble agreement scores.

llm_eval.what_worked: The agent identified a significant mismatch between model probability (0.02) and market price (0.43 mid), indicating substantial mispricing. The 41.1% entry edge is well-calculated and substantial. The forecast of 81.9F appears reasonable for late April context. Taking the NO side at 0.43 when model suggests 0.02 is directionally sound.
llm_eval.what_to_improve: Critical missing documentation: LLM confidence at entry and reasoning are unrecorded, making it impossible to assess decision quality beyond the edge calculation. The forecast (81.9F) needs context—is this from the agent's model or external source? Kalshi mid of 0.43 for a sub-2% probability event suggests either model miscalibration or market inefficiency that should be explicitly explained.
llm_eval.lesson: Strong edges don't validate process quality—document your confidence level and reasoning chain every trade to distinguish skill from luck, especially when model-market divergence is extreme.
llm_eval.avoid_next_time: Never leave LLM confidence and reasoning unrecorded. A 41% edge with a 112% win could be luck if confidence was low; always capture these inputs to enable true process review.

llm_eval.what_worked: Agent identified a meaningful discrepancy between model probability (0.25) and market price (0.58 mid), suggesting the market was overpricing the NO outcome. The 33.1% entry edge is quantifiable and the trade won decisively (+112%), validating the directional thesis.
llm_eval.what_to_improve: Missing critical documentation: LLM confidence not recorded at entry makes it impossible to assess whether the decision-making process was sound or lucky. The forecast of 83.6F appears reasonable for late April but lacks context on model calibration, forecast error bands, or confidence intervals. Should document why 0.25 model probability was chosen over other threshold estimates.
llm_eval.lesson: Document reasoning rigorously even on winning trades—edge quality cannot be verified without recorded confidence levels and model assumptions that justify the probability estimate.
llm_eval.avoid_next_time: Avoid trading without recording confidence metrics and model specifications. The ~33% edge is substantial but the sparse entry thesis makes it unclear if this was principled analysis or lucky direction guessing. This pattern prevents learning from wins.

llm_eval.what_worked: Agent identified a clear model-market divergence: 9% model probability vs 68% market price (implying 32% probability), providing statistical edge of 58.8%. The NO position was structurally sound given the low forecast (69.1F). Trade won decisively (+112%), suggesting directional conviction was correct.
llm_eval.what_to_improve: Missing critical documentation: LLM confidence not recorded at entry, which prevents assessing conviction calibration. No documented reasoning explains why the model probability (9%) was trusted or how forecast precision (69.1F specific) was validated. For a marginal 58.8% edge, deeper confidence documentation is essential.
llm_eval.lesson: Model-market divergence provides edge, but missing confidence metadata prevents learning; always record LLM confidence and reasoning rationale alongside statistical edge calculations.
llm_eval.avoid_next_time: Avoid executing trades with quantified edges (58.8%) while leaving confidence unrecorded—this creates a gap between process rigor and documentation that prevents post-trade analysis and confidence calibration.

raw_thesis: BTC spot $75,700 vs $77,999.99 threshold. Market priced YES at 78 cents (78% chance BTC closes above). Model: BTC ~$2,300 below threshold with 39 min to close. NO edge clear. New guardrails passed: 39 min > 30 min cutoff, buffer >0.5%, no momentum conflict.

llm_eval.what_worked: All post-April-21 guardrails passed cleanly. Filled as maker at 22 cents NO (actual cost $8.36 vs $29.64 estimate). BTC was well below threshold with sufficient time buffer.
llm_eval.what_to_improve: Consider whether maker-only strategy leaves too much alpha on the table when edge is strong enough to justify taker fill.
llm_eval.lesson: Fixed guardrails work. The same category of trade that lost on Apr 21 won cleanly on May 1 because the execution rules were tightened.

raw_thesis: RULE VIOLATIONS: (1) Model edge was -0.025 — negative, should have hard-blocked; (2) 9 min to expiry, under 30-min cutoff; (3) $49 buffer on $75K asset = 0.065%, under 0.5% minimum; (4) BTC had upward momentum all day. Order book NO accumulation was market-maker hedging, not real conviction.

llm_eval.what_to_improve: Edge filter must reject negative model edge. Near-expiry cutoff must block trades <30 min from close. Buffer check must enforce >0.5% spot-to-threshold distance. Momentum must agree with direction.
llm_eval.lesson: Four simultaneous rule violations in one trade. No single guardrail caught it because none were hard-enforced. Result: all four are now hard blocks in economics.py.
llm_eval.avoid_next_time: Negative edge = immediate reject. <30 min expiry on crypto = immediate reject. <0.5% buffer = immediate reject. Momentum conflict = immediate reject.

raw_thesis: GDPNow 1.31% > 1.0% threshold — this was the wrong side. NO on above 1.0% means betting GDP stays below 1.0%. GDP came in above 1.0% (1.5-2.0% range). Partial loss -$1.18 (-81%).

llm_eval.what_worked: Small position sizing limited damage to $1.18.
llm_eval.what_to_improve: GDPNow at 1.31% means GDP likely above 1.0% — NO on T1.0 was against the model. Should have been YES or no position.
llm_eval.lesson: Match thesis direction to model at every threshold. GDPNow above a threshold = YES, not NO.
llm_eval.avoid_next_time: Do not take NO on a threshold that GDPNow already exceeds.
